content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 12, 1992

Margaret A. McArthur
McArthur Productions
P. O. Box 189669
Sacramento, CA 95818

Re: Your Request for Confirmation
Of Telephone Advice
Our File No. I-92-335

Dear Ms. McArthur:

You have requested confirmation of telephone advice on behalf
of the California Channel concerning the lobbyist gift prohibition
contained in the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ You indicated that you are authorized to request this advice on behalf
of the California Channel, nor have you identified all of the
parties involved. Therefore, we treat your letter as a request
for informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329(c).2/

QUESTION

Is a reception sponsored by the California Channel and four
other organizations subject to the $10 lobbyist gift prohibition
by virtue of the fact that two lobbyists were listed on the
invitation?

CONCLUSION

As long as the lobbyists do nothing in connection with the
reception which constitutes making a gift of more than $10, or
arranging for the making of a gift of more than $10, to

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Margaret McArthur
Our File No. I-92-335
Page 2


legislative or state agency officials, being listed on an
invitation as a representative of a sponsor will not trigger the
lobbyist gift prohibition.

FACTS

The California Channel and four other sponsors have
contributed money to hold a reception. As I recall from our
telephone conversation, legislative officials have been invited to
the reception. When the invitations were printed, persons
representing the sponsors were also listed on the invitations,
including two lobbyists. However, all replies are being sent
directly to the public relations firm hired to put on the event,
and neither of the lobbyists are arranging for any part of the
event.

ANALYSIS

Section 86203 prohibits lobbyists from making or arranging
for the making of a gift of more than $10 in a calendar month to
any legislative official and to officials of state administrative
agencies which the lobbyist is registered to influence. (Sections
86201, 86203.) A lobbyist "arranges" for the making of a gift if
he or she, directly or through an agent, does any of the
following:

(a) Delivers a gift to the recipient.
(b) Acts as the representative of the donor, if
the donor is not present at the occasion of a gift.
This does not include accompanying the recipient to an
event where the donor will be present.
(c) Invites or sends an invitation to an intended
recipient regarding the occasion of a gift.
(d) Solicits responses from an intended recipient
concerning his or her attendance or nonattendance at the
occasion of a gift.
(e) Is designated as the representative of the
donor to receive responses from an intended recipient
concerning his or her attendance or nonattendance at the
occasion of a gift.
(f) Acts as an intermediary in connection with the
reimbursement of a recipient's expenses.

Regulation 18624.

Therefore, as long as neither lobbyist is making a payment
for the reception and neither lobbyist engages in any activity
which would constitute "arranging" as described above, benefits
provided to legislative and state agency officials at the
reception are not subject to the $10 lobbyist gift prohibition.

Margaret McArthur
Our File No. I-92-335
Page 3


     Please contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have questions
concerning this letter.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Scott Hallabrin
                                        Acting General Counsel

                                        [signature: Carla Wardlow]
                                   By:  Carla Wardlow
                                        Chief, Technical Assistance
                                             and Analysis Division
